campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 5, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review transportation funding investments from March 2026 and approved a new policy restricting how the city's property can be used by federal agencies. The council also discussed current job openings in the city and efforts to hire and keep good employees.
keyTopics: Santa Clara Valley Transportation Authority investment report for March 2026
New policy limiting federal agency use of city-owned property
City job vacancies and recruitment strategy
Employee retention efforts

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — April 15, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public hearing where residents could object to the city's plan to clean up hazardous materials on certain properties identified by the County. The council also adopted a new technology policy for the city and established a process to help residents request accommodations if they need special assistance.
keyTopics: Hazardous waste cleanup on local properties
New technology disruption policy
Process for residents to request reasonable accommodations

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — May 5, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on May 5, 2026 to approve meeting minutes and handle several property maintenance and service agreement issues. The council declared overgrown brush and hazardous vegetation as public nuisances and approved an engineer's report for landscape and lighting work. They also authorized the Town Manager to sign multi-year service contracts with various vendors.
keyTopics: Declared overgrown brush and hazardous vegetation as public nuisances
Approved landscape and lighting engineer's report
Authorized five-year service agreements with vendors
Approved previous meeting minutes from April 21, 2026

san-jose.title: San José City Council — May 12, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to approve meeting records and make updates to the city's banking arrangements with Wells Fargo. They also discussed a new program to help residents struggling with water bills and approved changes to a consulting contract for building services.
keyTopics: New financial assistance program to help residents pay water bills
Changes to city's banking agreement with Wells Fargo
Updates to consulting contract for building/facilities management

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — May 12, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The city council held a private meeting to discuss two sensitive matters: a legal issue related to a water main problem at Cuesta Park and negotiations to buy or lease two properties on Clyde Avenue. These topics were handled in closed session, meaning the public wasn't present, because they involved potential lawsuits and real estate negotiations.
keyTopics: Water main damage at Cuesta Park and possible legal claims
City negotiations regarding properties at 485 and 495 Clyde Avenue
Real estate acquisition or lease discussions
Potential litigation the city may face

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — May 5, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special closed-door meeting at 5 P.M. to discuss confidential matters, followed by a regular public council meeting at 7 P.M. The agenda highlights don't provide specific details about what issues were addressed in either session.
keyTopics: Closed session meeting (details not disclosed to public)
Regular council meeting convened at 7 P.M.
Small business focus mentioned in agenda

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — May 11, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held an initial planning session to discuss the city's budget for fiscal year 2026-27. The council also reminded the public about rules requiring anyone who lobbies the city on issues to register and report their activities.
keyTopics: FY 2026-27 budget planning and discussion
Lobbying registration and reporting requirements under municipal code
Public participation rules for testimony and submissions

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — May 11, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara's City Council held a meeting on May 11, 2026 focused on economic development and marketing for the city. The council is continuing to offer both in-person and remote participation options so residents can attend and provide input however works best for them.
keyTopics: Economic Development & Marketing Committee meeting
In-person and remote (Zoom) attendance options available
Public comment submissions via email by 11 AM on meeting day
Ongoing city economic development initiatives

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — May 5, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to plan the city's finances and development for the next several years. They discussed the budget for 2026-27, approved a plan for new parks and trails in the Innovation District, and updated their internal meeting procedures.
keyTopics: 5-year capital improvement plan (2027-2031) - money for infrastructure projects
2026-27 operating budget presentation - how the city will spend money next fiscal year
Innovation District Parks and Trails Master Plan - new outdoor spaces coming to the area
Updates to City Council procedures and meeting rules

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — May 7, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The city council held a hearing about a plan to build a 6-story apartment building with 72 units at 450 Lytton Avenue, replacing a city parking lot. The project would be 100% affordable housing and is asking the city for special approvals to make the project feasible under state affordable housing laws.
keyTopics: 72 new affordable housing units planned for downtown Lytton Avenue location
City parking lot (Lot T) would be removed to make room for the development
Developer requesting density bonuses and waivers under California affordable housing law
6-story building on small 0.43-acre parcel in compact urban format
